title: westringia rubiifolia
intro: Westringia rubiifolia, also known as native rose or coastal rosemary, is a flowering shrub native to Western Australia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever and cough. The leaves and flowers of Westringia rubiifolia have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Westringia rubiifolia can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
